signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + 40-Year High
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 163.15, marking a fresh 40-year high as Middle East tensions and a +2.44% surge in WTI oil prices ($84.5) drive broad USD demand. The pair remains structurally supported by a +275bp OIS carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.98%) and a bear flattener yield curve regime. Despite the breakout, the 25d risk reversal at -1.42 (Saxo) shows a moderate put skew, signaling that the options market is paying for protection against a sharp reversal rather than chasing the spot extension. JPY COT LF net short -105,238 confirms the carry trade is heavily crowded, while the USD/JPY vs VIX correlation (z=-1.73σ) indicates the JPY's traditional safe-haven function is currently broken. Price holds comfortably above the SMA20/50/200 cluster (162.05/160.62/157.46), leaving the upside open until verbal intervention or a VIX spike above 25 forces a deleveraging event. Risk: BoJ verbal intervention or a surprise hawkish shift at the July 31 meeting triggers a disorderly short-squeeze.

signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Cabinet Shock
signals.text: GBP/USD trades at 1.3375, underperforming its G10 peers following high-impact headlines regarding UK cabinet changes and a moderate negative CESI score (net -8). The G10 scorecard triggers a bilateral theme warning with a 0.803% spread between the leading USD (+0.385%) and the laggard GBP (-0.418%). While the pair holds a marginal +14bp OIS carry advantage over the USD, the technical position is deteriorating as price tests the SMA50 at 1.3378. COT LF net long positioning remains elevated at +28,278 (64.8% long), suggesting the pair is vulnerable to further long-unwinding if political uncertainty persists. A sustained break below the SMA20/50 cluster (1.3343/1.3378) would shift the technical bias toward the SMA200 at 1.3400. Risk: BoE hawkishness at the July 30 meeting offsets political risk and stabilizes the GBP.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Tariff Shock + COT-Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4105, driven by a TIER-1 tariff shock as the U.S. imposes 50% duties on selected Canadian goods. This fundamental headwind is amplified by a +150bp OIS carry advantage favoring the USD (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.29%). Speculative positioning is heavily short CAD (LF net -93,515, 20.6% long), while retail sentiment shows a 71% short bias on USD/CAD, creating a significant divergence where retail is fading the institutional trend. The USD/CAD vs WTI correlation break (z=-1.85σ) confirms that trade policy and carry are currently overriding the traditional oil link. Price remains below the SMA20 (1.4154) but above the SMA50 (1.3990), suggesting a consolidation phase before the next leg higher. Risk: A reversal in U.S. tariff rhetoric or a significant beat in Canadian domestic data.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD holds at 0.7003, supported by a -76bp OIS carry deficit for the USD (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) and a hawkish RBA bias. However, the pair faces headwinds from a strong negative CESI score (net -13), indicating persistent data misses that contradict the central bank's stance. Options markets are pricing an elevated vol premium with IV at 11.4% vs HV30 at 6.5% (VRP +4.9%), reflecting high hedging demand for downside protection. COT LF net long positioning at +21,946 (63.2% long) suggests the market is already positioned for RBA hawkishness, leaving little room for upside surprises. Price is currently pinned between the SMA20 (0.6941) and SMA50 (0.7046). Risk: RBA shifts to a neutral bias at the August 11 meeting, triggering a liquidation of spec longs.
